_comment: DATED price history for FAITHFUL per-run-date cost accounting. prices.json stays the runtime/current snapshot the engine bills against; this is the analysis source of truth for what a past run actually cost. Each model maps to a list of windows; a window's rates take effect on `effective` (UTC date, inclusive) and apply until the next window's `effective`. RULE: a genuine provider PRICE CHANGE adds a new dated window (old runs keep the old rate); a CORRECTION to a wrong past table EDITS the rates of the window that covers the affected dates (the provider's real rate that day wasn't what we'd recorded). The most-recent window per model should equal prices.json — costs.py warns if they drift. Optional `long_context` applies the higher tier to the WHOLE request when input_tokens exceed `threshold` (per OpenAI's >272K gpt-5.5 tier; the long-context cache_read here is an assumption = 0.1x long-context input). Tau Ceti's only table change so far (TauCetiReview#50) was a correction of a stale table while provider rates were stable, so each model currently has a single window at its true rate effective from before the project began.
